備註: (n) a disease that exists in almost all of an area

(a) (of a disease) existing in almost all of an area
Ex: In some parts of the world malaria is still pandemic.
例如：在世界某些地區，瘧疾仍然流行。

(n) a type of virus that causes diseases in humans and animals. In humans, it usually causes 
respiratory infections (= in the nose, throat, or chest) that are not serious
Ex: SARS was a new strain of coronavirus.
例如：SARS是冠狀病毒的一種新株。
(n) the top rounded part of a body part such as a tooth or the skull,

(a) caused by a virus, 
Ex: Within days the film clip went viral
例如：幾天之內，這段影片就迅速走紅。

(n) a serious illness in which one or both lungs become inflamed and fill with fluid, 
Ex: The doctor checked whether the patient had pneumonia.
例如：醫生檢查了病人是否有肺炎。

(n) any feeling of illness or physical or mental change that is caused by a particular disease
Ex: He's complaining of all the usual flu symptoms - a high temperature, headache, and so on
例如：他抱怨自己出現了所有常見的流感症狀──高燒、頭痛等等。

(n) a condition in which body temperature is higher than normal,
Ex: A high fever is a common symptom of infection
例如：高燒是感染的常見症狀。

(n) to force air out of your lungs through your throat with a short, loud sound
Ex: The smoke made me cough
例如：煙霧讓我咳嗽

(n) extreme tiredness, especially caused by illness or hard work
Ex: Many patients reported fatigue after infection.
例：許多患者在感染後表示感到疲倦。

(n) the ability to notice odors with the nose; a particular odor,

Ex: Some people temporarily lose their sense of smell.
例如：有些人暫時失去了嗅覺。

(a) related to the nose, 
Ex: nasal passages, 鼻道
Ex: nasal congestion, 鼻塞

(n) a situation in which a place is too blocked or crowded, causing difficulties, 
Ex: These facilities are designed to ease the congestion of emergency rooms
例如：這些設施旨在緩解急診室的擁擠狀況。

(n) a painful infection of the eyes that makes them red and makes the eyelids swollen 
and sticky,

(a) painful and uncomfortable because of injury, infection, or too much use, 

Ex: All the dust has made my eyes sore.
例如：灰塵讓我的眼睛很痛。
Ex: sore throat.
例如：喉嚨痛。

(n) a pain in the head.
Ex: She had a headache and a sore throat
例如：她頭痛又喉嚨痛。

(n) one of the tissues in the body that makes movement possible.
Ex: COVID-19 may cause muscle pain.
例如：新冠病毒感染可能導致肌肉疼痛。

(n) a place in your body where two bones are connected
Ex: an elbow/hip/knee joint, 肘/ 髖/ 膝關節

(n) an illness in which the body's solid waste is more liquid than usual and comes out of the body more often, 
Ex: an attack of diarrhoea, 腹瀉發作

(n) a lot of small red spots on the skin, 
Ex: I've got an itchy rash all over my chest.
例如：我胸口長滿了癢癢的皮疹。

(n) a feeling of cold, 
Ex: There was a chill in the air this morning.
例如：今天早上空氣中瀰漫著一絲寒意。

(n) a temporary feeling that your sense of balance is not good and that you may fall down, 
Ex: The sight of blood may cause dizziness in some people
例如：有些人看到血會感到頭暈。

(n) the feeling that you are going to vomit
Ex: Signs of the illness include fever, nausea, and vomiting.
例如：這種疾病的症狀包括發燒、噁心和嘔吐。

(n) the act of bringing food or liquid up from the stomach through the mouth, 
Ex: Severe nausea may lead to vomiting.
例如：嚴重的噁心可能導致嘔吐。

中文:
非常嚴重的；劇烈
的；慘重的
備註:
(a) causing very great pain, difficulty, worry, damage, etc.; very serious, 
Ex: In parts of Africa there is a severe food/water shortage.
例如：非洲部分地區嚴重缺糧/缺水。

(n) difficulty breathing normally, 
Ex: Shortness of breath can be a sign of severe disease.
例如：呼吸急促可能是嚴重疾病的徵兆。

(n) the feeling that you want to eat food, 
Ex: All that walking has given me an appetite.
例：走了這麼多路，我胃口大開。

中文: 困惑，糊塗;迷惑不解;混淆
備註:
(n) a situation in which people do not understand what is happening, what they should do or who someone or something is, 
Ex: There seems to be some confusion over who is actually giving the talk
例如：似乎有人搞不清楚到底是誰在演講。

(n) the upper front part of the body of humans and some animals, between the stomach and the neck, containing the heart and lungs, 
Ex: He was shot in the chest at point blank range.
例：他被近距離射中胸部。

(n) the quality of becoming annoyed very easily, 
Ex: The drug can cause irritability
例如：這種藥物會引起煩躁。

(n) the state of understanding and realizing something, 
Ex: Her consciousness that she's different makes her feel uneasy
例如：她意識到自己與眾不同，這讓她感到不安。

(n) a feeling of worry, nervousness, or fear, 
Ex: Long illness can cause anxiety in some patients.
例如：長期患病會導致一些患者焦慮。

(n) the state of feeling very unhappy and without hope for the future, 
Ex: I was overwhelmed by feelings of depression
例如：我當時感到非常沮喪。

(n) a state of untidiness or lack of organization, 
Ex: The whole office was in a state of disorder.
例：整個辦公室一片混亂。

(a) relating to nerves, 
Ex: Alzheimer's disease is a neurological disorder.
例如：阿茲海默症是一種神經系統疾病。

併發症
使情況複雜化的事物；複雜化
備註:
(n) an extra medical problem that makes it more difficult to treat an existing illness, 併發症
(n) 使情況複雜化的事物；複雜化
Ex: If there are no complications, the doctor says that she'll be able to come home within two weeks.
例如：如果沒有併發症，醫生說她兩週內就能回家。

(n) a sudden change in the blood supply to a part of the brain, sometimes causing a loss of the 
ability to move particular parts of the body, 
Ex: She suffered/had a stroke that left her unable to speak.
例如：她中風了，導致她無法說話。

(n) a red, painful, and often swollen area in or on a part of your body,
Ex: Aspirin reduces pain and inflammation
例如：阿斯匹靈可以減輕疼痛和發炎。

中文: （因發燒或精神錯亂而）神志不清;語無倫次
備註:
(n) a state of being unable to think or speak clearly because of fever or mental confusion, 

Ex: fever accompanied by delirium
例如：高燒伴有神志不清

(v) to become well again after an illness, 
Ex: Most people recover without hospital treatment.
例：大多數人無需住院治療即可康復。

(n) the way you deal with or behave towards someone or something, 
Ex: Peter gets special treatment because he knows the boss.
例如：彼得因為認識老闆而受到特殊待遇。

(n) a gas in the air that people and animals need to breathe, 
Ex: Some seriously ill patients require oxygen.
例如：有些重症患者需要吸氧氣。
(a) relating to breathing,

(n) a dangerous condition in which the lungs cannot provide enough oxygen to the body, 
Ex: Respiratory failure needs immediate medical care.
例如：呼吸衰竭需要立即就醫。

(a) If a bad situation is acute, it causes severe problems or damage, 
Ex: She felt acute embarrassment at his behaviour.
例如：她對他的行為感到非常尷尬。

Ex: Early diagnosis of the syndrome is difficult, but it is important to begin treatment as soon as possible
例如：這種綜合徵的早期診斷很困難，但盡快開始治療至關重要。
(n) a severe medical condition in which bacteria enter the blood after an operation or accident,

(n)

例如：醫師會在嚴重病例中密切觀察血栓栓塞的發生情形。

(a) real but not immediately obvious, 
Ex: And what might be the underlying significance of these supposedly random acts?
例如：這些看似隨機的行為背後可能隱藏著什麼意義？

(n) either of the two organs in the chest with which people and some animals breathe, 
Ex: lung cancer
例：肺癌
